label: Iron-recycling macrophage
evidence: The atlas paper Gopee et al. (2024) explicitly names 'iron-recycling' as one of four macrophage subsets in prenatal skin (alongside LYVE1+, MHCII+, and TML macrophages). The macrophage subset annotations were derived from marker genes from Suo et al. (2022). The term 'iron-recycling macrophage' appears in figure legends (Extended Data Fig. 8c) and in the main text section 'Macrophages support prenatal skin angiogenesis'.
